name: Cisco AppDynamics APM & Observability API
what_it_does: Cisco AppDynamics REST API for enterprise full-stack application performance monitoring and business observability platform. Enables AI agents to manage application topology and dependency map retrieval for impact analysis, handle metric query and time-series data retrieval for performance monitoring, access transaction snapshot and call graph data for bottleneck analysis, retrieve health rule and policy alert event processing, manage business transaction monitoring and SLA tracking, handle database and infrastructure monitoring integration data, access Business iQ for correlating APM data with business KPIs, retrieve anomaly detection and baseline deviation events, manage synthetic monitoring and user journey data, and integrate AppDynamics performance data with ITSM, SIEM, and cloud management platforms.
use_cases: Querying application performance metrics from SRE automation agents
Processing health rule violations from incident response agents
Mapping application topology for impact analysis from NOC agents
Correlating APM data with business KPIs from Business iQ agents
not_for: SMB and startup monitoring without enterprise scale and complexity requirements
Simple web app monitoring without full-stack distributed tracing needs
Consumer application performance without enterprise SLA and licensing
best_when: An enterprise using Cisco AppDynamics wants AI agents to automate performance monitoring, health rule event processing, transaction analysis, infrastructure correlation, and integration with ITSM and SIEM platforms.
avoid_when: COMPLEXITY WARNING: AppDynamics has a large API surface with controller-based deployment — on-premises controllers require network access; SaaS deployment requires different auth flows. Business iQ and analytics require separate licensing. Agent instrumentation complexity means not all application tiers are equally well-monitored. Enterprise licensing cost is significant.

auth.notes: AppDynamics uses OAuth 2.0 (client credentials) for SaaS and API key for on-premises controller. Controller-scoped API with application, tier, and node hierarchy. Documentation at docs.appdynamics.com. HTTP actions for webhook integration. Cisco Intersight integration available. Enterprise SSO and LDAP integration. Controller version matters for API capabilities.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise licensing with annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-agent annual licensing; APM, Infrastructure, Business iQ, and Synthetic separately priced
pricing.notes: San Francisco, California. Founded 2008. Acquired by Cisco (2017, $3.7B). Enterprise APM market leader. 3,500+ global customers. Now part of Cisco Observability Platform (FSO). Business iQ correlation of APM with business outcomes differentiator. Strong in financial services and retail. Competes with Dynatrace and Datadog for enterprise APM.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; documented per controller version

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLEXITY: AppDynamics controller hierarchy (controller → application → tier → node) requires navigation through multiple API levels; understand data model before automation
On-prem vs. SaaS auth difference — on-premises controllers use API key; SaaS uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials; verify deployment type before integrating
Controller version dependency — API capabilities vary by controller version; verify API endpoint availability against your specific controller version
Business iQ licensing — Business iQ analytics API requires separate license; not included in standard APM subscription
Metric baseline — AppDynamics uses dynamic baselines for anomaly detection; new applications need 1-2 weeks before meaningful anomaly detection is available
Cisco FSO transition — AppDynamics is transitioning to Cisco Full-Stack Observability platform; verify current API and roadmap alignment for new integrations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise APM. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP, GDPR. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Application topology, performance traces, and business data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise APM and observability. SOC2, FedRAMP. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Application performance and business intelligence data.
